topic: Why your SoundCloud link isn't enough
hook: Sending a SoundCloud link to a music supervisor is like sending a screenshot of your CV to a hiring manager.
cta: Save this. Build the package. Then reach out.
caption_hook: Sending a SoundCloud link to a music supervisor is like ↓
caption_full:
Sending a SoundCloud link to a music supervisor is like ↓

It tells them you exist.

Save this. Build the package. Then reach out.

#SyncMaster #AfricanComposers #SyncLicensing #MusicBusiness #SyncReady

slides.headline: Sending a SoundCloud link to a music supervisor is like

slides.body:
It tells them you exist. That's all. ↓ ✓ Rights status confirmed
One-stop or clearly defined split, documented before you pitch.

Here's what a supervisor actually needs to move your track forward: ✓ Stems available
Not now. But ready when they ask.

✓ High-quality WAV or AIFF
Not a stream. Not a download link. A file, ready immediately. SoundCloud tells a supervisor:
→ A track exists
→ You have a profile

✓ Proper file naming
Format: ArtistName_TrackTitle_BPM_Key.wav
"finalfinalv3_mixdown.wav" doesn't cut it. That's not enough to move a placement forward.

✓ Embedded metadata
Title, composer, ISRC, mood, genre, BPM — inside the file, not in an email.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
